title: Melted Butter Syrian Ice Cream Biryani
description: A unique Syrian dessert featuring buttery, bouncy ice cream and spherified flavors, this dish is Jain-friendly and perfect for warm evenings. It combines traditional Syrian ingredients with a modern twist of ice cream and spherification.
instructions: Rinse the basmati rice thoroughly and soak it in water for 30 minutes.
In a large pot, heat the ghee over medium heat and add the soaked rice, stirring constantly.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pot.
Simmer the rice for 15 minutes or until it is cooked and fluffy.
In a separate bowl, mix the ice cream with cardamom powder and rose water until well combined.
Using a syringe or spoon, drop small amounts of the ice cream mixture into a bath of water containing sodium alginate, creating small spheres.
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the sugar and melted ice cream spheres.
Serve the melted butter Syrian ice cream biryani hot, garnished with additional rose water and cardamom powder if desired.
